reasoning: The occurrence of a new coronavirus pandemic declared by the WHO within a 10-month window of a bat coronavirus discovery is historically and biologically extremely unlikely. Given the absence of search tools to discover an ongoing outbreak as of October 2025, I must rely heavily on the base rate. Such events happen less than once per several decades (even considering the increased attention post-COVID-19). Furthermore, prediction markets on such 'tail risk' hypothetical outbreak questions overwhelmingly trade at floor probabilities unless there is already massive global news of a novel pathogen out of control. Without such evidence, I constrain my estimate to the lower bound of the available range (2%).
